qa_pairs.question: What is Ansys Rocky used for?
qa_pairs.answer: Ansys Rocky is a software for engineering simulation, specifically for fluids, which can run simulations in both graphical user interface (GUI) and non-GUI modes.

qa_pairs.question: What computing resources does Ansys Rocky support for running simulations?
qa_pairs.answer: Ansys Rocky supports running simulations with either CPUs only or with a combination of CPUs and GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: Which Ansys module should be used for Rocky simulations on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should use Rocky included within the `ansys/2025R1` module or newer versions available on all clusters. The `ansysrocky/2024R2.0` module is deprecated and will be removed.

qa_pairs.question: How can I view a complete list of command-line options for Ansys Rocky?
qa_pairs.answer: You can get a full listing of command-line options for Rocky by running `Rocky -h` on the command line after loading the appropriate Rocky module.

qa_pairs.question: Is it necessary to configure the PATH environment variable for Rocky to be found?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is currently necessary to set your PATH to Rocky, as shown in the provided scripts, for the `Rocky` command to be found. This path will be included in future module version installs and ansys/2025R1 updates.

qa_pairs.question: How many CPUs should be requested for GPU-based coupled problems when running Rocky?
qa_pairs.answer: When Rocky is being run with GPUs to solve coupled problems, the number of CPUs requested from Slurm (on the same node) should be increased to a maximum until the scalability limit of the coupled application is reached.

qa_pairs.question: How many CPUs are recommended for GPU-based standalone uncoupled problems with Rocky?
qa_pairs.answer: If Rocky is being run with GPUs to solve standalone uncoupled problems, then only a minimal number of CPUs (e.g., 2 or possibly 3) should be requested that will allow Rocky to still run optimally.

qa_pairs.question: What type of licenses are required if Rocky is run with four or more CPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: When Rocky is run with four or more CPUs, `rocky_hpc` licenses will be required, which the SHARCNET license does provide.

qa_pairs.question: Have the provided Slurm scripts for Rocky been recently tested?
qa_pairs.answer: The provided Slurm scripts for Rocky have not been tested since the clusters were updated across the Alliance in Fall 2025, so some adjustments may be required.

qa_pairs.question: How do you run Ansys programs in graphical mode?
qa_pairs.answer: To run Ansys programs in graphical mode, you can click on one of the provided OnDemand or Jupyterhub links, which will open a job submission web page to configure resources for an interactive session in your browser.

qa_pairs.question: What are the web links for accessing Ansys in graphical mode on NIBI, FIR, RORQUAL, NARVAL, and TRILLIUM?
qa_pairs.answer: The web links are: NIBI: `https://ondemand.sharcnet.ca`, FIR: `https://jupyterhub.fir.alliancecan.ca`, RORQUAL: `https://jupyterhub.rorqual.alliancecan.ca`, NARVAL: `https://jupyterhub.narval.alliancecan.ca/`, and TRILLIUM: `https://ondemand.scinet.utoronto.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after clicking an OnDemand or Jupyterhub link for graphical Ansys use?
qa_pairs.answer: After clicking one of these links, a job submission web page will appear in your browser, prompting you to configure the resources needed for an interactive session.

qa_pairs.question: How do you start an Ansys application graphically if you've opened a Jupyter Lab launcher interface?
qa_pairs.answer: If you started a Jupyter Lab launcher interface, you can load an Ansys software module from the left-side menu, then click one of the Ansys icons (e.g., for CFX, Fluent, MAPDL, or Workbench) to start the application.

qa_pairs.question: How do you start an Ansys application graphically if you've started a Compute or Basic Desktop from the Nibi OnDemand system?
qa_pairs.answer: If you've started a Compute or Basic Desktop from the Nibi OnDemand system, you will need to open a terminal window, manually load an Ansys module, and then type the program name to start it from the command line.

qa_pairs.question: What type of GPU resource should be requested for Ansys applications that require accelerated graphics in an OnDemand Desktop session?
qa_pairs.answer: For applications like Fluent that require accelerated graphics for 3D rendering, a whole GPU resource (such as H100 or T4 at the time of writing) should be requested.
